skill.name: pdf
skill.description: Comprehensive PDF manipulation toolkit for extracting text and tables, creating new PDFs, merging/splitting documents, and handling forms. When Claude needs to fill in a PDF form or programmatically process, generate, or analyze PDF documents at scale.
skill.summary: Comprehensive PDF manipulation toolkit for extracting text and tables, creating new PDFs, merging/splitting documents, and handling fillable forms.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 219 patterns across 12 files, but all are false positives. The 'external_commands' are markdown documentation code blocks, not executable code. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' warnings are from test files using MD5 for non-security test data generation. 'C2 keywords' and 'system reconnaissance' are CLI argument parsing and legitimate system calls. The skill performs legitimate PDF processing using Python libraries (pypdf, pdfplumber, reportlab). Low risk due to filesystem operations and external tool dependencies (poppler-utils, qpdf) that require user-provided file paths.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Write Operations
low_findings.description: Scripts write output PDF and JSON files to disk. User controls file paths via CLI arguments, minimizing risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed file write operations in Python scripts using open() with user-provided paths. Low risk because paths are CLI arguments, not user input from untrusted sources.

low_findings.title: External Tool Dependencies
low_findings.description: Documentation references external CLI tools (qpdf, pdftotext, pdfimages) that execute shell commands. These are documented usage patterns, not embedded malicious code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation shows legitimate use of standard PDF utilities (poppler-utils, qpdf). Commands are examples for users to run manually, not automated execution.

content.value_statement: Handle PDF forms, extract text and tables, merge or split documents, and create new PDFs programmatically. This skill eliminates manual PDF editing by automating form filling, data extraction, and document assembly tasks.
actual_capabilities: Extract text and tables from PDF documents using pdfplumber
Fill PDF forms programmatically with field value mapping
Merge multiple PDFs into single documents or split PDFs by page
Create new PDFs from scratch using reportlab
Add text annotations to non-fillable PDF forms with precise positioning
Extract and analyze PDF form field definitions and annotations
limitations: Requires Python libraries (pypdf, pdfplumber, reportlab) to be installed
OCR for scanned PDFs requires additional tools (pytesseract, pdf2image)
Command-line tools (qpdf, poppler-utils) must be available for some operations
Cannot modify existing PDF content, only add annotations or create new documents

use_cases.title: Automated Form Processing
use_cases.description: Extract field definitions from fillable PDF forms, map user data to fields, and generate completed forms without manual entry.

use_cases.title: Document Data Extraction
use_cases.description: Extract structured text and tables from PDF reports, invoices, or documents for analysis and conversion to other formats.
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts working with PDF source documents

use_cases.title: PDF Assembly and Generation
use_cases.description: Merge multiple PDFs, split large documents, add watermarks, or create new PDFs from templates programmatically.

prompt_templates.title: Extract PDF Form Fields
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a fillable PDF form at [path]. Extract all form field information including field names, types, and locations. Save the output to a JSON file so I can map values to each field.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Analyzing a fillable PDF to understand its structure

prompt_templates.title: Fill PDF Form with Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Fill the PDF form at [input.pdf] using the values in [field_values.json]. The form has fields for name, address, and checkboxes. Validate that all fields are mapped correctly before generating the output.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Populating a known form with structured data

prompt_templates.title: Extract Tables from PDF Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all tables from this financial report PDF. Convert each table to a pandas DataFrame and save them to an Excel file with separate sheets. Preserve column headers and data types where possible.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Data extraction from structured documents

prompt_templates.title: Add Text Annotations to Non-Fillable Form
prompt_templates.prompt: This PDF form has no fillable fields. Analyze each page to identify where text should be entered. Create bounding boxes for labels and entry areas, generate validation images, and add text annotations at the correct positions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Handling scanned or static forms requiring visual analysis

output: Validating field IDs against PDF form...

best_practices: Always validate bounding boxes visually before adding annotations to non-fillable forms
Test PDF operations on a copy before processing original documents
Specify explicit font sizes when adding text annotations to ensure proper rendering
anti_patterns: Do not skip validation steps when working with non-fillable forms
Avoid using default file paths; always specify explicit input and output locations
Do not assume all PDFs have fillable fields; check before attempting form filling

faq.question: What Python libraries does this skill require?
faq.answer: The skill uses pypdf for basic PDF operations, pdfplumber for text and table extraction, and reportlab for PDF creation. Install with: pip install pypdf pdfplumber reportlab

faq.question: Can this skill fill out scanned PDF forms?
faq.answer: Yes, but it requires a visual analysis step. The skill converts PDF pages to images, identifies field locations with bounding boxes, validates positions, then adds text annotations at the correct coordinates.

faq.question: How do I handle PDFs with no fillable fields?
faq.answer: Follow the non-fillable form workflow: convert to images, create a fields.json with label and entry bounding boxes, generate validation images to verify positions, then add annotations using the fill script.

faq.question: What external tools are needed for command-line operations?
faq.answer: For CLI operations, install poppler-utils (provides pdftotext, pdfimages) and qpdf. On Ubuntu: apt install poppler-utils qpdf. On macOS: brew install poppler qpdf

faq.question: Can this skill extract tables from PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes, using pdfplumber. The skill can extract tables as structured data and convert them to pandas DataFrames or Excel files for further analysis.

faq.question: How are checkbox and radio button values handled?
faq.answer: For fillable forms, the skill extracts the checked/unchecked values from the PDF. For checkboxes, use the checked_value to mark them. For radio groups, select one of the radio_options values.
